---
title: "tbox-finder — Phase 1 (Backbones & heads) dev-log"
author: "bioedca"
format:
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

<!-- Rendered with the Typst engine (quarto ≥1.9, `format: typst`): the
     laptop/CI toolchain ships no LaTeX, and Typst produces the PDF natively.
     Renders via `conda run -n tbox-viz quarto render analyses/phase1_log.qmd`. -->


# Phase 1 — Backbones & heads

Per-step development log (CLAUDE.md §2.4). One stanza is appended after every
step's commit; this file renders to `docs/dev-log/phase1_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf` at
the phase-exit gate.

## Step P1-01 — 2026-07-12: Stage-1 CUDA-kernel import/forward smoke gate + pure-PyTorch fallback cost

**Inputs.** The pinned GPU env **`tbox-ml-dna`** (`envs/ml-dna.yml` + `ml-dna.conda-lock.yml`:
torch 2.7.1+cu128, mamba-ssm 2.2.6.post3, causal-conv1d 1.6.2.post1, flash-attn 2.8.3.post1);
the P0-06 `sm_86` wheel-availability finding (ADR-0002 A2); a seeded random-DNA token fixture
(`tests/fixtures/kernel_smoke_dna/dna_tokens.npy`, seed 42, `[2,512]` int8).

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/kernels/kernel_smoke.py` (run via `slurm/p1/kernel_smoke.sbatch` on
one cluster RTX A4000, Ampere `sm_86`, driver 590.48.01) probes the runtime, asserts
`import selective_scan_cuda` (ADR-0002 A2 C2), embeds the DNA fixture into selective-scan inputs
(fp32; D=512, N=16, L=512, B=2), and forwards through both the CUDA kernels
(`selective_scan_fn`/`causal_conv1d_fn`) and the hand-wired pure-PyTorch references
(`selective_scan_ref`/`causal_conv1d_ref`), recording kernel-vs-ref max-abs parity and tokens/sec.
Kernel APIs were verified against the library source at the pinned tags (v2.2.6.post3 / v1.6.2.post1)
**and** the installed env (context7 quota-blocked). The first run (job 460) exposed a partial
`causal-conv1d` install in the cluster env (orphan `.so`, no package/dist-info) — repaired by
reinstalling the exact pinned wheel; the smoke was hardened to record import failures in-report
rather than crash (the job-460 crash left no report).

**Outputs.** `reports/p1/kernel_smoke.json` (git_sha `8ba3880`, SLURM job 462, `measured=true`);
`src/tbox_finder/kernels/{__init__,kernel_smoke}.py`; `slurm/p1/kernel_smoke.sbatch`;
`tests/unit/test_kernel_smoke_report.py` (14 cases, CPU-only); `tests/fixtures/kernel_smoke_{dna,sample}/`;
**ADR-0002 Amendment A5**.

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** (`gate.overall_pass=true`): import + forward green on an A4000 `sm_86`
(`selective_scan_cuda` + `causal_conv1d_cuda` + mamba-ssm/causal-conv1d/flash-attn all import);
`selective_scan` parity **1.53e-5** and `causal_conv1d` **9.5e-7** (both ≪ `atol` 1e-2); measured
pure-PyTorch `selective_scan` fallback throughput ratio **≈871×** (CUDA 12.46 M vs ref 14.3 K tok/s);
`causal_conv1d` **0.56×** (the D3 optional accelerator — small-tensor launch overhead, correctness is
what the gate needs). Artifact-based verification (`sacct` off): `squeue` empty + `kernel_smoke.DONE`
present + zero-byte `.err`.

**Decisions.** The "pure-PyTorch fallback" is `selective_scan_ref` invoked directly — the packaged
`Mamba` block never reaches it (ADR-0002 A2 C2), so the ≈871× slowdown quantitatively confirms the
prebuilt `sm_86` wheel is **load-bearing**, not a convenience. `atol` is an implementer choice (ADR-0002
pins no kernel-vs-fallback tolerance; the raw diff is always recorded, §10.3). Cross-link **ADR-0002 A5**.

**Commit.** `8ba3880` (scaffold `5f18180` + hardening `8ba3880`) + the follow-up carrying this stanza,
the report, and A5; squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in `TODO.md` at P1-01 close.

## Step P1-02 — 2026-07-12: Load Caduceus-PS default checkpoint + human-reference pretraining provenance

**Inputs.** The default Stage-1 checkpoint — Caduceus-PS
`kuleshov-group/caduceus-ps_seqlen-131k_d_model-256_n_layer-16` (7.73 M params, `d_model` 256,
16 MambaDNA layers, RC-equivariant) — pinned at the **immutable commit** `d89eeb85…` (never `main`);
the `tbox-ml-dna` env (transformers 4.57.5 `trust_remote_code` ceiling; ADR-0002 D2/A4); a short
seeded DNA fixture + its reverse-complement built in token space via the config `complement_map`.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/models/caduceus_backbone.py::load_caduceus_ps` loads the backbone
(`AutoModel`, `trust_remote_code=True`) at the pinned revision on the **local GPU** (RTX 4060, `sm_89`),
counts parameters, and measures RC-equivariance — `f(RC(x))` vs `f(x).flip(dims=(-2,-1))` (the
model-card hidden-state RC transform) — with a reverse-only negative control. The CLI writes the
canonical `data/interim/caduceus_ps_131k/provenance.json` sidecar (`provenance.py`; ADR-0002 as the
pinning ADR). **imp.md↔ADR reconciliation:** imp.md's "pure-PyTorch CPU forward" is superseded by
ADR-0002 A2 C2 (the packaged `Mamba` hard-imports `selective_scan_cuda`; no CPU forward exists) —
resolved via the local GPU (still LOCAL, no SLURM). Loader/RC APIs verified against the installed
transformers 4.57.5 + the HF model card (context7 quota-blocked).

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/{__init__,caduceus_backbone}.py`; `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml`;
`data/interim/caduceus_ps_131k/provenance.json` (git-tracked, `measured=true`, git_sha `bd64aa6`);
`tests/unit/test_caduceus_backbone.py` (23 cases: 21 bare + 2 GPU). **No DVC artifact** (metadata
sidecar; weights are pulled from HF by revision, not stored).

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** (`gate.overall_pass=true`): loads at the pinned revision; **param count
== 7,725,312** (== `EXPECTED_PARAM_COUNT`, the PRD "7.73 M" to the exact integer); **RC-equivariance
max-abs-diff 0.0** ≤ `atol` 1e-4 (bit-exact), reverse-only negative control **2.39** (the check
discriminates); provenance carries `pretraining.domain = "human reference genome"` + ≥2 citations + the
`ml-dna` env-lock hash. Bare tier **21 passed / 2 skipped** (the CI path); GPU tier green under
`tbox-ml-dna` (§8.5 manual gate); `TBOX_REQUIRE_CADUCEUS=1` rot-guard fails closed; full unit suite
**396 passed / 53 skipped** (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean.

**Decisions.** The checkpoint is pinned by an **immutable commit SHA** (never `main`, ADR-0002 D2); the
param count + equivariance are **frozen in code** (no config can weaken them — the coverage.py
`OOD_ECE_MIN_N` precedent). RC-equivariance is asserted on the **hidden states** (what the P1-03 head
consumes) via the model-card `flip(dims=(-2,-1))` transform — the §6 strand-resolver precondition.
**Scientific-evidence gate (§10.1) CLEARED** for the load-bearing human-reference pretraining fact:
≥2 authoritative agreeing sources — HF model card (verbatim) + Schiff et al. (arXiv:2403.03234;
PMID:40567809), both already in-repo (ADR-0002). The gate is **hardware-agnostic** (`sm_89` laptop;
RC-equivariance is an architectural property of RCPS, identical on the cluster A4000 `sm_86`).
Cross-link **ADR-0002 D7**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-02-caduceus` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-02 close); feeds **P1-03** (seg-head build), **P1-04** (frozen-embedding probe),
**P1-09** (continued-pretraining).
